chapter_summary: Jesus calls His first disciples — Simon Peter, James, and John — after a miraculous catch of fish. He heals a man covered with leprosy with a touch. He heals a paralysed man lowered through the roof by his four friends. He calls Levi (Matthew) the tax collector. He teaches about new wine and new wineskins.
main_lesson: Jesus calls ordinary people to follow Him and transforms their lives. Whether you are a fisherman, a sick person, or someone others look down on — Jesus calls you, heals you, and gives you a fresh start.

memory_verse.kjv: And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
memory_verse.little_bible: Jesus said to Simon: 'Don't be afraid! From now on, instead of catching fish, you will be catching people — bringing them to know God.'

parent_guide: Luke 5 opens the discipleship narrative. The miraculous catch (v1-11) is a commissioning story — note Peter's response to the miracle: 'Depart from me, for I am a sinful man.' Awareness of holiness produces the right kind of fear. Jesus' response is not condemnation but call: 'Fear not.' The leper episode (v12-16) is remarkable — lepers were untouchable, and Jesus touches him. Physical touch was forbidden; Jesus reverses this entirely. The paralytic episode (v17-26) shows Jesus' authority to forgive sins AND heal bodies — the Pharisees are right that only God can forgive sins; they draw the wrong conclusion about what this proves. Levi's calling (v27-32) is the third consecutive calling — another person considered unworthy. The new wine image (v33-39) signals that Jesus brings something entirely new that cannot simply be added to the old system.
application_for_children: Jesus called fishermen, healed a man no one would touch, and had dinner with people everyone else avoided. He calls YOU too — just as you are. When you follow Jesus, He uses your life in ways you never expected!

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, that, as the people pressed upon him to hear the word of God, he stood by the lake of Gennesaret,
verses.little_bible: Crowds of people pushed in close to Jesus because they wanted to hear God's Word. Jesus stood beside the beautiful Lake of Gennesaret, where the fishermen worked.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Lots of people crowded around Jesus to hear Him. He stood by a big lake.
verses.meaning: The people were so hungry for God's Word that they pressed in around Jesus. He met them right where they were — at the edge of an ordinary working lake.
verses.memory_phrase: People pressed in to hear God's Word.
verses.prayer: God, give me a hungry heart that always wants to hear what You say.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever really, really wanted to hear something? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: Why do you think ordinary people were so eager to hear Jesus? What made His words different from anyone else's?
verses.do_it_today: Find one quiet moment today to sit and read a few verses of the Bible — just to listen to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: A shimmering blue lake at dawn with rolling hills behind it, crowds of people in colourful robes pressing toward the water's edge where Jesus stands calmly. Storybook watercolour style.

verses.kjv: And saw two ships standing by the lake: but the fishermen were gone out of them, and were washing their nets.
verses.little_bible: Jesus saw two fishing boats pulled up at the shore. The fishermen had climbed out of them and were washing their nets after a long night of work.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Two boats were by the lake. The fishermen were washing their nets.
verses.meaning: The fishermen had worked all night and were done for the day — but God was just getting started with them. Jesus walked into their ordinary morning.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus walks into ordinary moments.
verses.prayer: Jesus, come into my ordinary day and do something amazing.
verses.discussion_question: What do you do every morning? Could Jesus be part of that?
verses.family_discussion: These men were just doing their everyday job. How does God sometimes meet us in the middle of our ordinary routines?
verses.do_it_today: As you do something normal today — eating, walking, washing up — say a short prayer and invite Jesus in.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two wooden fishing boats resting on calm lake water at early morning, fishermen in simple clothes pulling dripping nets over the sides. Soft golden sunrise light.

verses.kjv: And he entered into one of the ships, which was Simon's, and prayed him that he would thrust out a little from the land. And he sat down, and taught the people out of the ship.
verses.little_bible: Jesus climbed into Simon's boat and asked him to push it out a little from the shore. Then Jesus sat down and taught the crowd from the boat, with the water all around Him.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus got into Simon's boat, pushed out from shore, and taught the people.
verses.meaning: Jesus used what was available — a fisherman's boat — to accomplish His purpose. Simon offered what he had and Jesus made it into something extraordinary.
verses.memory_phrase: Offer what you have to Jesus.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I give You what I have — my time, my things, my life. Use it for Your work.
verses.discussion_question: What is something you own that you could share or use to help others?
verses.family_discussion: Simon gave Jesus his boat without being told why. What does it look like to offer what we have to God before we know the outcome?
verses.do_it_today: Give one thing to someone today — your time, your help, or something you own.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus sitting calmly in a wooden fishing boat a little way out from shore, water glittering around Him, teaching to a crowd on the bank who lean forward to listen. Warm morning light.

verses.kjv: Now when he had left speaking, he said unto Simon, Launch out into the deep, and let down your nets for a draught.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus had finished teaching, He said to Simon: 'Now row out into the deep water and drop your nets to catch some fish!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: When Jesus finished, He said: 'Row to the deep water and drop your nets!'
verses.meaning: Jesus moved from teacher to Lord in a moment. His command to go to the deep was a test of trust — would Simon obey even when it made no sense?
verses.memory_phrase: Go where Jesus tells you to go.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You tell me to do something that seems strange, help me trust and obey.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever asked you to do something that seemed a bit silly, but it turned out to be a great idea?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told a professional fisherman to go back out after an unsuccessful night. When has God asked your family to try again when you felt like giving up?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you gave up on? Ask God if He wants you to try again.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus in the boat pointing toward the deep open water, Simon at the oars looking uncertain but listening. The deep water ahead shimmers and the shore is far behind.

verses.kjv: And Simon answering said unto him, Master, we have toiled all the night, and have taken nothing: nevertheless at thy word I will let down the net.
verses.little_bible: Simon sighed and said: 'Master, we worked hard all through the night and didn't catch a single fish. But — because You say so — I'll put the net down again.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Simon said: 'We caught nothing all night. But if You say so, I'll try again.'
verses.meaning: Simon's reply is one of the most beautiful examples of faith in the Gospels — 'at thy word I will.' He obeyed not because it made sense, but because Jesus said it.
verses.memory_phrase: At Your word, I will.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I choose to trust Your word even when it doesn't make sense to me. At Your word, I will.
verses.discussion_question: When is it hard to trust someone? How do you decide whether to trust them?
verses.family_discussion: 'At thy word I will' — make this a family motto. When was the last time your family did something simply because God's Word said so, even when it was hard?
verses.do_it_today: Say these words today: 'Jesus, at Your word, I will ____________.' Fill in the blank with something He's been asking you to do.
verses.illustration_prompt: Simon looking tired and a little doubtful, but nodding as he holds a net, with Jesus watching him calmly and kindly from the front of the boat. Early morning mist on the water.

verses.kjv: And when they had this done, they inclosed a great multitude of fishes: and their net brake.
verses.little_bible: When they let the net down, it filled up with so many fish that the net started to break! There were more fish than they had ever seen in one catch!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They dropped the net and caught so many fish the net started breaking!
verses.meaning: Obedience to Jesus produced something impossible. The catch was so large it went beyond what their equipment could handle — a miracle overflowing with abundance.
verses.memory_phrase: Obedience to Jesus overflows with blessing.
verses.prayer: God, help me obey You and trust that You will do more than I expect.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever got more of something than you expected? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: The miracle came only after Simon obeyed. What might your family be missing because there is something God has asked that hasn't been obeyed yet?
verses.do_it_today: Do one thing today that you have been putting off — something you sense God has been nudging you toward.
verses.illustration_prompt: A bulging fishing net straining under the weight of silvery fish leaping and splashing, the net nearly splitting, two men pulling with all their strength. Dramatic, joyful, sunlit water.

verses.kjv: And they beckoned unto their partners, which were in the other ship, that they should come and help them. And they came, and filled both the ships, so that they began to sink.
verses.little_bible: Simon waved and shouted to his partners in the other boat to come quickly! They rowed over and helped — and they filled both boats so full of fish that the boats started to sink!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They waved to their friends for help. Both boats filled up with fish — almost sinking!
verses.meaning: Good things are meant to be shared. The miracle was too big for one boat. God's blessings overflow and involve the whole community.
verses.memory_phrase: God's blessings are too big to hold alone.
verses.prayer: God, when You bless me, help me share it with others around me.
verses.discussion_question: When something really good happens to you, who is the first person you want to tell or share it with?
verses.family_discussion: The fishermen called for help immediately when the blessing was too big. How does your family share your blessings with others?
verses.do_it_today: Share something today — food, a compliment, or help — with someone who needs it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two overloaded wooden boats sitting very low in the water, both crammed with shining fish, fishermen laughing and struggling to balance, water nearly reaching the rim. Bright joyful watercolour.

verses.kjv: When Simon Peter saw it, he fell down at Jesus' knees, saying, Depart from me; for I am a sinful man, O Lord.
verses.little_bible: When Simon Peter saw what had happened, he fell down at Jesus' feet. He said: 'Lord, go away from me — I am a sinful man. I am not good enough to be near You!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Simon fell at Jesus' feet and said: 'I am sinful, Lord. I shouldn't be near You!'
verses.meaning: A true encounter with Jesus always shows us who we really are. Peter's response — falling down — was not just fear but the right kind of worship before holiness.
verses.memory_phrase: Seeing Jesus truly shows us ourselves.
verses.prayer: Lord, I know I am not perfect. But I come to You just as I am. Please don't go away from me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever felt like you weren't good enough to be near someone very important? How did that feel?
verses.family_discussion: Peter saw his own sin when he was close to Jesus' holiness. Is that a bad thing? What is the right response when we see our own failures before God?
verses.do_it_today: Tell God honestly today about one thing you know is wrong in your heart. Don't run away — run toward Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Simon Peter kneeling in the fish-filled boat, face down at Jesus' feet, overwhelmed and humbled. Jesus standing above him, calm and majestic, light around Him. Tender and powerful.

verses.kjv: For he was astonished, and all that were with him, at the draught of the fishes which they had taken:
verses.little_bible: Simon was completely astonished — and so was everyone else who was with him — at the enormous catch of fish they had pulled in.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Simon and everyone with him were amazed at all the fish.
verses.meaning: The miracle was undeniable. Everyone on those boats was a witness. What Jesus does cannot be explained away — it leaves people astonished.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus leaves people amazed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, amaze me today. Do something in my life that only You could do.
verses.discussion_question: What is something that has happened in your life that you couldn't explain?
verses.family_discussion: The fishermen were professionals — they knew what was normal and what wasn't. Why is it important that it was experienced fishermen, not beginners, who were astonished?
verses.do_it_today: Talk with your family about one time God did something you couldn't explain. Praise Him for it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Fishermen in two boats staring open-mouthed at the mass of fish around them, hands on cheeks, eyes wide. Sunlight pouring over the scene, the lake shimmering.

verses.kjv: And so was also James, and John, the sons of Zebedee, which were partners with Simon. And Jesus said unto Simon, Fear not; from henceforth thou shalt catch men.
verses.little_bible: James and John — Zebedee's sons, who worked with Simon — were just as amazed. Then Jesus said to Simon: 'Don't be afraid! From now on you will catch people instead of fish — bringing them to know God!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said to Simon: 'Don't be afraid! Now you will catch people for God!'
verses.meaning: Jesus did not condemn Peter for his sin. He called him. 'Fear not' is always Jesus' answer to the right kind of fear. The miracle was a preview of the mission.
verses.memory_phrase: Fear not — Jesus calls you.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You don't turn away from me. Instead, You call me closer and give me a purpose.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been afraid of something but then found out it was actually going to be amazing?
verses.family_discussion: Peter said 'go away' and Jesus said 'fear not, come with Me.' What does that tell us about how Jesus responds when we feel we are too sinful to be near Him?
verses.do_it_today: Tell one person today something good about Jesus — you are already 'catching people' when you do!
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus placing His hand gently on Simon Peter's shoulder in the boat, looking at him with warmth and purpose. James and John watching from the second boat. Morning light on calm water.

verses.kjv: And when they had brought their ships to land, they forsook all, and followed him.
verses.little_bible: When they had brought the boats back to shore — boats full of the best catch of their lives — they left everything behind and followed Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They brought the boats to shore and left everything to follow Jesus.
verses.meaning: The most amazing catch of fish they ever had, and they walked away from it all. When Jesus calls, He becomes more valuable than everything else combined.
verses.memory_phrase: They left everything and followed Him.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me love You more than anything else I have. You are worth everything.
verses.discussion_question: What is your most treasured possession? What would it mean to love Jesus more than that?
verses.family_discussion: The disciples left a boat full of fish — a fortune — to follow Jesus. Is there anything your family is holding too tightly that might be getting in the way of following Jesus more fully?
verses.do_it_today: Put something you treasure in a special place today and say: 'Jesus, You are worth more than this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Three fishermen walking away from their boats on the shore, nets and fish left behind, following Jesus up a sun-drenched path. Their backs are to the boats; their faces look toward the horizon.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass, when he was in a certain city, behold a man full of leprosy: who seeing Jesus fell on his face, and besought him, saying, Lord, if thou wilt, thou canst make me clean.
verses.little_bible: In another city, a man came to Jesus who was covered all over with a terrible skin disease called leprosy. He fell flat on his face before Jesus and said: 'Lord, if You are willing, I know You can make me clean!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: A man covered in leprosy fell before Jesus and said: 'Lord, if You want to, You can make me clean!'
verses.meaning: The man had no doubt about Jesus' power — only about His willingness. His faith was real, his humility was real, and his desperation was real. He brought all of it to Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Lord, if You are willing, You can.
verses.prayer: Lord, I know You can do anything. I trust Your willingness too. Whatever You decide, I trust You.
verses.discussion_question: If you could ask Jesus to fix one thing, what would it be?
verses.family_discussion: The man said 'if thou wilt' — he trusted Jesus' power but submitted to His will. How do we pray believing God can help, while also trusting His answer, even if it's not what we expected?
verses.do_it_today: Pray about one hard thing today and add: 'But Lord, I trust Your will in this.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A man in tattered robes covered in skin sores lying face down before Jesus on a dusty city street. People keeping their distance in the background, but Jesus stepping toward the man. Tender storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And he put forth his hand, and touched him, saying, I will: be thou clean. And immediately the leprosy departed from him.
verses.little_bible: Jesus reached out His hand and touched the man — something nobody had done in years! He said: 'I am willing. Be clean!' And instantly, the leprosy was completely gone.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus reached out and touched him. He said: 'I am willing! Be clean!' And the leprosy was gone at once.
verses.meaning: Lepers were untouchable — the law forbade contact. Jesus broke every social barrier and touched the man before healing him. The touch said: 'You are not untouchable to Me.'
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus touches the untouchable.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that no one is too broken or too far away for You to reach. You touched him, and You can touch me too.
verses.discussion_question: Has anyone ever made you feel like you didn't belong? How does it feel to know Jesus would reach out to you?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus could have healed the man without touching him — He did it with a word in other healings. Why do you think He chose to touch this man?
verses.do_it_today: Reach out to someone today who seems lonely or left out. A kind word or a smile can be a 'touch' from Jesus through you.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus kneeling down, extending His hand to touch the face of the man on the ground. At the moment of touch, the sores on the man's skin are visibly fading. Soft golden light radiating from the touch. Deeply tender scene.

verses.kjv: And he charged him to tell no man: but go, and shew thyself to the priest, and offer for thy cleansing, according as Moses commanded, for a testimony unto them.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told him firmly: 'Don't tell anyone yet. First, go to the priest and show yourself, and make the offering that Moses' law requires — that will be a witness to everyone that you are truly clean.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't tell anyone yet. Go show the priest you are healed.'
verses.meaning: Jesus respected God's law. By sending the man to the priest, He was following the proper procedure for declaring someone healed of leprosy — honouring the system God had set up.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus honours God's instructions.
verses.prayer: God, help me do things the right way, even when it would be easier to skip steps.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of a time you had to do things in the right order even when you were excited?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus told the man to follow the official process. How do we balance enthusiasm and excitement with doing things properly and with integrity?
verses.do_it_today: Do one task today the right and careful way from start to finish — don't skip any steps.
verses.illustration_prompt: The newly healed man standing tall, skin completely clear, receiving quiet instructions from Jesus who speaks to him gently and privately. Warm, peaceful street scene in the background.

verses.kjv: But so much the more went there a fame abroad of him: and great multitudes came together to hear, and to be healed by him of their infirmities.
verses.little_bible: But even so, the news about Jesus spread more and more! Huge crowds gathered from all around to hear Him teach and to be healed of their sicknesses.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: News about Jesus spread everywhere and huge crowds came to hear Him and be healed.
verses.meaning: You cannot keep a miracle secret for long. Jesus' reputation spread not through His own promotion but through the lives He changed.
verses.memory_phrase: Changed lives spread the news.
verses.prayer: God, let my changed life tell others about You without me even having to say much.
verses.discussion_question: What is the best way to tell others about something wonderful — with words or by showing them?
verses.family_discussion: The healed man's changed life attracted people to Jesus. How does the way your family lives attract others to want to know God?
verses.do_it_today: Do something kind, honest, or generous today where others can see it — and let it point to Jesus.
verses.illustration_prompt: A wide landscape scene with streams of people from different directions all walking toward a glowing point in the distance where Jesus stands. Rolling hills and morning sky.

verses.kjv: And he withdrew himself into the wilderness, and prayed.
verses.little_bible: But Jesus would often slip away from the crowds and go somewhere quiet — out into the wilderness — just to pray and be alone with His Father.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus often went away to a quiet place to pray.
verses.meaning: The busier Jesus was, the more He prayed. This pattern in His life is a model for all of us — prayer is not what you do when everything else is done; it is what makes everything else possible.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus always found time to pray.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me make prayer a habit — not just something I do when things go wrong.
verses.discussion_question: Where is your favourite quiet place? Could that become a place for you to pray?
verses.family_discussion: The most powerful person who ever lived made time to pray. What does that tell us about how important prayer should be in our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Find one quiet spot today — even for five minutes — and just talk to God with no distractions.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus alone in a rocky wilderness landscape at dawn, kneeling on a stone, head bowed in prayer. Soft morning colours — pink and gold. Total stillness and peace.

verses.kjv: And it came to pass on a certain day, as he was teaching, that there were Pharisees and doctors of the law sitting by, which were come out of every town of Galilee, and Judaea, and Jerusalem: and the power of the Lord was present to heal them.
verses.little_bible: One day Jesus was teaching and Pharisees and teachers of the law had come from every town — all the way from Galilee, Judaea, and Jerusalem. And the power of the Lord was right there, ready to heal.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus was teaching and religious leaders from everywhere had come. God's power was there to heal.
verses.meaning: The official religious establishment had come to observe and judge. But God's healing power was present regardless — because it depends on Jesus, not the audience.
verses.memory_phrase: God's power is present to heal.
verses.prayer: Lord, Your power is not stopped by people who doubt. Come and do what only You can do.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever tried to do something good while someone was watching and judging you? How did you handle it?
verses.family_discussion: Even with critics in the room, Jesus taught and God's power was present. How do we stay faithful when people are watching and waiting for us to fail?
verses.do_it_today: Do the right thing today even if someone is watching or judging. Let God's power show up in your faithfulness.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus teaching in a packed room, stern Pharisees in formal robes seated in a row on one side, ordinary people crowded together on the other. A sense of tension and expectation in the air.

verses.kjv: And, behold, men brought in a bed a man which was taken with a palsy: and they sought means to bring him in, and to lay him before him.
verses.little_bible: Then some men arrived carrying their friend on a stretcher — he was completely paralysed and couldn't walk. They were determined to find a way to get him in front of Jesus!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Some men carried their paralysed friend on a mat to get him to Jesus.
verses.meaning: Love finds a way. These friends refused to let a crowd stop them. Their determination was a picture of real, active friendship.
verses.memory_phrase: Real friends carry each other to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, help me be the kind of friend who helps others get to You.
verses.discussion_question: What makes someone a really good friend?
verses.family_discussion: These men saw a need and took action — carrying their friend through a crowd. Who in your community needs someone to 'carry' them right now?
verses.do_it_today: Pray for one friend or family member today who needs help. Then do something practical for them.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four determined friends carrying a man on a woven mat through a busy crowded street, weaving between people, faces set with purpose. Warm dusty colours, bustling background.

verses.kjv: And when they could not find by what way they might bring him in because of the multitude, they went upon the housetop, and let him down through the tiling with his couch into the midst before Jesus.
verses.little_bible: The crowd was too thick — there was no way through! So they climbed up to the flat roof, moved some tiles, made a hole, and carefully lowered their friend on his mat — right down into the middle of the room, right in front of Jesus!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: They couldn't get inside, so they went to the roof, made a hole, and lowered their friend down to Jesus!
verses.meaning: Nothing — not a wall of people, not a roof — was going to stop these friends. Their creativity and determination were fuelled by love for their friend and faith in Jesus.
verses.memory_phrase: Nothing stops real faith.
verses.prayer: Lord, help me find a way to reach You even when there are obstacles in my path. Give me faith that doesn't give up.
verses.discussion_question: If you really needed to get somewhere but the door was blocked, what would you do?
verses.family_discussion: The friends destroyed part of someone's roof to bring healing to their friend. How much inconvenience or effort are we willing to accept to bring others to Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: If something is blocking you from connecting with God today — a habit, a distraction, a fear — remove it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Four friends on a flat rooftop, tiles removed, carefully lowering a man on a mat through the hole with ropes. Dust drifts down into a room below where Jesus sits surrounded by people. Dramatic overhead view.

verses.kjv: And when he saw their faith, he said unto him, Man, thy sins are forgiven thee.
verses.little_bible: When Jesus saw the faith of all five of them — the man and his four friends — He said: 'Friend, your sins are forgiven.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw their faith and said: 'Friend, your sins are forgiven!'
verses.meaning: Jesus saw the faith before He saw the illness. And what He addressed first was not the paralysis but the deeper problem — sin. This tells us what He values most.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus sees your faith.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe in You. Please forgive all my sins, just like You forgave this man.
verses.discussion_question: Why do you think Jesus forgave the man's sins before fixing his legs?
verses.family_discussion: Which is greater — a healed body that will one day die, or sins forgiven and a soul that lives forever? What does this verse say about Jesus' priorities?
verses.do_it_today: Ask Jesus to forgive you for something specific today. Then believe that He has.
verses.illustration_prompt: The paralysed man on the mat in the middle of the room looking up in surprise at Jesus, who smiles at him with deep compassion. Dust still settling from the hole above. Light streaming down from the opening in the roof.

verses.kjv: And the scribes and the Pharisees began to reason, saying, Who is this which speaketh blasphemies? Who can forgive sins, but God alone?
verses.little_bible: The scribes and Pharisees started whispering to each other: 'Who does this man think he is? Only God can forgive sins! This is blasphemy!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The religious leaders whispered: 'Only God can forgive sins! Who does He think He is?'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees were right that only God can forgive sins. They were wrong about what that meant — because God was standing right in front of them.
verses.memory_phrase: Only God can forgive sins — and Jesus is God.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I believe that You are God and that You have the power to forgive me completely.
verses.discussion_question: If someone said they could forgive everyone in the world for everything, what would you think?
verses.family_discussion: The Pharisees asked the right question but came to the wrong conclusion. How do we make sure we don't read the Bible and still miss who Jesus really is?
verses.do_it_today: Write this truth somewhere today: 'Jesus is God. He can forgive my sins.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A row of serious scribes and Pharisees in formal robes leaning toward each other, whispering with alarmed expressions. Everyone else in the room leans forward toward Jesus with hope.

verses.kjv: But when Jesus perceived their thoughts, he answering said unto them, What reason ye in your hearts?
verses.little_bible: Jesus immediately knew what they were thinking — even though they hadn't said a word out loud. He looked at them and said: 'What are you arguing about in your hearts?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus knew what they were thinking and said: 'Why are you arguing in your hearts?'
verses.meaning: Jesus can see our hidden thoughts. He does not need anyone to inform Him. This is both a comfort and a challenge — our inner life is known to Him.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus knows what we think inside.
verses.prayer: Lord, You see every thought I have. Help me think thoughts that please You.
verses.discussion_question: Does it change how you behave if you know someone can see everything you do?
verses.family_discussion: What would change in our daily lives if we lived with the awareness that Jesus sees and hears our thoughts?
verses.do_it_today: Before your next thought becomes an action, pause and ask: 'Would Jesus be pleased with this thought?'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus turning to look directly at the whispering Pharisees with calm, knowing eyes. The religious leaders look startled, as if caught. Light centred on Jesus' face.

verses.kjv: Whether is easier, to say, Thy sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Rise up and walk?
verses.little_bible: Jesus asked them: Which is easier to say — your sins are forgiven, or get up and walk? Jesus was about to prove He had power to do both.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus asked: Is it easier to forgive sins or to heal someone? He was about to show both.
verses.meaning: It is easy to say 'your sins are forgiven' because no one can immediately see if it happened. 'Get up and walk' can be tested on the spot. Jesus was about to give visible proof of invisible power.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus proves what He says.
verses.prayer: God, I trust Your Word even when I cannot see the proof yet. Help my faith to grow.
verses.discussion_question: How do you know if something is really true? What makes you believe it?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus connected forgiving sins with healing the body — to prove He had authority over both. How does this help us trust that our sins really are forgiven when Jesus says so?
verses.do_it_today: When Jesus says something in the Bible, choose to believe it is true today — even if you can't see it.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus holding out both hands — one open toward the man on the mat, one toward the Pharisees — as if presenting a choice. The room hushed in expectation.

verses.kjv: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power upon earth to forgive sins, (he said unto the sick of the palsy,) I say unto thee, Arise, and take up thy couch, and go into thine house.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'I want you all to know that the Son of Man has authority right here on earth to forgive sins!' And He turned to the paralysed man and commanded: 'Get up! Pick up your mat and go home!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I have power to forgive sins!' Then He told the man: 'Get up and go home!'
verses.meaning: Jesus gave them visible proof of invisible authority. To prove He could forgive sins, He did something everyone could see — and no one could deny.
verses.memory_phrase: The Son of Man has authority on earth.
verses.prayer: Jesus, You are the Son of Man with all authority. I trust every word You speak over my life.
verses.discussion_question: If someone said they had a power that was invisible, how would you know it was real?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus performed a public miracle to confirm a spiritual truth. How does this help us trust that what Jesus says about forgiveness is real and not just words?
verses.do_it_today: Say out loud: 'Jesus has the authority to forgive my sins, and I believe He has.'
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus standing tall and commanding in the centre of the packed room, pointing at the man on the mat, the crowd watching in complete silence. The Pharisees look uncertain. Light seems to radiate from Jesus.

verses.kjv: And immediately he rose up before them, and took up that whereon he lay, and departed to his own house, glorifying God.
verses.little_bible: Immediately the man jumped to his feet! He rolled up the mat he had been lying on, walked right through the crowd, and went home — praising God the whole way!
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Right away the man got up, picked up his mat, and walked home praising God!
verses.meaning: The healing was instant, total, and public. There was no doubt and no delay. And the man's first response was to glorify God — exactly the right response to a miracle.
verses.memory_phrase: Healed! Up! Walking! Praising God!
verses.prayer: God, when You do something amazing for me, let my first response always be to praise You.
verses.discussion_question: When something really good happens to you, what do you do first?
verses.family_discussion: The healed man's response was immediate praise. What keeps us from being quick to thank and praise God when He acts in our lives?
verses.do_it_today: Praise God out loud today for something specific He has done for you — don't wait.
verses.illustration_prompt: A man striding joyfully upright through a parted crowd, a rolled mat on his shoulder, arms raised slightly, face beaming upward. People on both sides stunned, some reaching out to touch him in amazement. Warm bright light.

verses.kjv: And they were all amazed, and they glorified God, and were filled with fear, saying, We have seen strange things to day.
verses.little_bible: Everyone was astonished. They all praised God and were filled with awe, saying: 'We have seen extraordinary things today — things we have never seen before!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Everyone was amazed and praised God, saying: 'We have seen incredible things today!'
verses.meaning: Even the critics were silenced. When God's power is at work, the only right response is wonder, worship, and awe. 'Strange things' means things beyond the ordinary — truly extraordinary.
verses.memory_phrase: We have seen extraordinary things.
verses.prayer: God, let me never stop being amazed by You. You are always doing things beyond what I can expect.
verses.discussion_question: What is the most extraordinary thing you have ever seen?
verses.family_discussion: The crowd was 'filled with fear' — meaning holy awe. Is that the same as being scared? How would you describe the difference?
verses.do_it_today: Tell someone today about one extraordinary thing God has done. Share your awe.
verses.illustration_prompt: A room full of people — all expressions of shock, wonder, and worship. Some with hands raised, some with mouths open, some on their knees. The healed man walking through them all. Golden light fills the room.

verses.kjv: And after these things he went forth, and saw a publican, named Levi, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he said unto him, Follow me.
verses.little_bible: After that, Jesus went out and saw a tax collector named Levi sitting at his tax booth. Jesus looked right at him and said: 'Follow Me.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus saw a tax man named Levi and said: 'Follow Me.'
verses.meaning: Tax collectors were hated — they worked for Rome and often cheated their own people. Jesus walked straight past everyone else and called the most unlikely person. Grace knows no boundaries.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus calls the most unlikely people.
verses.prayer: Jesus, thank You that You call people no one else would choose. Thank You for calling me.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been chosen for something when you didn't expect it? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: Levi collected taxes for a foreign empire — he was considered a traitor. Why do you think Jesus chose to call him? What does that say about who Jesus came for?
verses.do_it_today: Include someone today who might not normally be chosen or noticed.
verses.illustration_prompt: Levi sitting at a stone tax table with coins stacked around him, looking up in surprise as Jesus stands before him with a hand outstretched and a warm, direct gaze. People in the background watching in shock.

verses.kjv: And he left all, rose up, and followed him.
verses.little_bible: And right then — Levi stood up, left all his money and his tax booth behind, and followed Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Levi got up, left everything, and followed Jesus.
verses.meaning: No hesitation. No negotiation. No 'let me finish this first.' Levi's immediate response shows that when Jesus truly calls us, He makes following Him more compelling than anything we leave behind.
verses.memory_phrase: He left all and followed.
verses.prayer: Jesus, when You call me, help me respond quickly and completely — not hold anything back.
verses.discussion_question: If Jesus said 'follow Me right now,' what would you need to leave behind?
verses.family_discussion: Levi had a job, income, and security — and he left it all in one moment. What does immediate obedience look like in everyday life for your family?
verses.do_it_today: Is there something you have been putting off doing for God? Do it today. Don't wait.
verses.illustration_prompt: Levi rising from his seat at the tax table, coins scattering, leaving his ledger and money behind, stepping toward Jesus who waits ahead. The scene feels sudden and joyful, like a new chapter beginning.

verses.kjv: And Levi made him a great feast in his own house: and there was a great company of publicans and of others that sat down with them.
verses.little_bible: Levi threw a huge celebration feast at his house for Jesus! He invited all his friends — other tax collectors and many others — and they all sat and ate together with Jesus.
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Levi had a big party for Jesus at his house and invited all his friends!
verses.meaning: Levi's first act after following Jesus was to introduce Jesus to everyone he knew. His table became a place of grace. The best response to being called is to bring others in.
verses.memory_phrase: Levi brought everyone to Jesus.
verses.prayer: God, help me want to bring the people I love to know You the way Levi did.
verses.discussion_question: When something amazing happens to you, who are the first people you want to tell or celebrate with?
verses.family_discussion: Levi immediately used his connections and his home for Jesus. What resources, friendships, or spaces does your family have that could be used to welcome people to know Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Invite someone to something — a meal, church, a family gathering. Be like Levi and open your life to others.
verses.illustration_prompt: A long joyful feast in a warm torchlit home — Levi at the centre laughing, Jesus among a crowd of tax collectors and friends eating, drinking, and talking. Warm reds and golds, festive and full of life.

verses.kjv: But their scribes and Pharisees murmured against his disciples, saying, Why do ye eat and drink with publicans and sinners?
verses.little_bible: But the Pharisees and scribes grumbled to Jesus' disciples: 'Why do you eat and drink with tax collectors and sinners? That's not right!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: The Pharisees complained: 'Why does Jesus eat with bad people?'
verses.meaning: The Pharisees defined holiness as separation from sinful people. Jesus defined it as going toward them with love. Their criticism reveals their misunderstanding of God's heart.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus goes toward, not away.
verses.prayer: God, help me not to judge people who are different or who have made mistakes. Help me to love like Jesus.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever been left out because of what people thought of you? How did it feel?
verses.family_discussion: The Pharisees kept their distance to stay 'holy.' Jesus went close to love people. Which approach actually changes lives?
verses.do_it_today: Sit with or talk to someone today who is often ignored or excluded.
verses.illustration_prompt: Pharisees in white robes standing outside the house peering through the window with disapproving frowns at the joyful feast inside. Stark contrast of cold outside and warm light inside.

verses.kjv: And Jesus answering said unto them, They that are whole need not a physician; but they that are sick.
verses.little_bible: Jesus answered: 'Healthy people don't need a doctor — sick people do!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Healthy people don't need a doctor. Sick people do!'
verses.meaning: Jesus used a simple, unassailable image. No one argues with this logic. His point: He is not the doctor for people who think they are well — He is for those who know they need help.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the doctor for those who know they're sick.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know I need You. Thank You for being my healer.
verses.discussion_question: Do you ever feel like you need help? What kind of help?
verses.family_discussion: Knowing we need Jesus is the starting point of everything. What keeps us from admitting we need Him as much as we do?
verses.do_it_today: Tell Jesus one real need you have today and let Him be your doctor.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus gesturing simply and clearly to the Pharisees, one hand open, calm and confident. Behind him through the doorway, the warm feast continues.

verses.kjv: I came not to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'I didn't come to call people who think they are already good enough. I came to call people who know they have done wrong — and invite them to turn around and follow God.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'I came to call sinners to turn back to God — not people who think they are already good.'
verses.meaning: This verse states Jesus' mission clearly. Repentance means turning around — changing direction. Jesus calls people not to stay as they are but to be made new.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus came to call sinners.
verses.prayer: Jesus, I know I need to turn to You. I am turning now. Thank You for calling me.
verses.discussion_question: What does it mean to 'turn around' when you are going the wrong way?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus didn't come for people who think they are righteous on their own. What is the danger of thinking we are good enough without Jesus?
verses.do_it_today: Admit one thing to God today where you have been going the wrong way — and turn toward Him.
verses.illustration_prompt: Jesus speaking warmly to the Pharisees outside, one arm gesturing back to the joyful dinner inside. His posture is open and inviting. The contrast between His welcome and their coldness is clear.

verses.kjv: And they said unto him, Why do the disciples of John fast often, and make prayers, and likewise the disciples of the Pharisees; but thine eat and drink?
verses.little_bible: Some people challenged Jesus: 'John's disciples fast and pray often, and so do the Pharisees' disciples — but Your disciples just eat and drink! Why?'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: People asked: 'Others fast and pray a lot, but Your followers just eat. Why?'
verses.meaning: The questioners were comparing outward religious habits. But Jesus was inaugurating something entirely new — and new seasons have new expressions of devotion.
verses.memory_phrase: Not all seasons of faith look the same.
verses.prayer: God, help me connect with You in real and living ways — not just go through the motions.
verses.discussion_question: Do you know why people fast (go without food to pray)? Have you ever tried it?
verses.family_discussion: Religion can become about performing habits rather than knowing God. How do you keep your family's faith alive and real rather than routine?
verses.do_it_today: Do one act of devotion today that is real and personal — not just a habit.
verses.illustration_prompt: A small group of people standing outside speaking to Jesus with puzzled, slightly accusing faces. Some pointing toward other groups of people kneeling and praying in the distance.

verses.kjv: And he said unto them, Can ye make the children of the bridechamber fast, while the bridegroom is with them?
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'Would wedding guests go without food while the groom is still there celebrating with them? Of course not!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'You don't fast at a wedding while the groom is right there with you!'
verses.meaning: Jesus is the bridegroom — being with Him is like being at the greatest wedding celebration ever. His presence calls for joy, not mourning.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus is the bridegroom — rejoice!
verses.prayer: Jesus, knowing You is my reason for joy. Help me celebrate Your presence every day.
verses.discussion_question: What do you imagine a wedding celebration is like? What makes it special?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus compared His presence to a wedding feast. How can your family cultivate an atmosphere of joy and celebration because Jesus is with you?
verses.do_it_today: Do something joyful today to celebrate the fact that Jesus is alive and with you.
verses.illustration_prompt: A joyful wedding feast scene — flowers, music, laughter, bright colours — with Jesus at the centre surrounded by friends. The scene feels like the best kind of celebration.

verses.kjv: But the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken away from them, and then shall they fast in those days.
verses.little_bible: Then Jesus said: 'But the time will come when the groom is taken away from them. When that happens, then they will fast — and that will be the right time.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'But one day I will be gone. Then it will be time to fast and mourn.'
verses.meaning: Jesus was quietly foretelling His death. Fasting has its place — but the time and reason matter. Everything must be connected to reality, not just religious performance.
verses.memory_phrase: Every season has its right response.
verses.prayer: God, help me respond to what You are doing right now — in this season — with a real and alive heart.
verses.discussion_question: Can you think of something that is the right thing to do at one time but not at another?
verses.family_discussion: Jesus was hinting at His death even here. How do we hold together the joy of knowing Jesus now with the serious weight of what He went through to save us?
verses.do_it_today: Spend a quiet moment today thinking about what Jesus went through for you — and let it move you.
verses.illustration_prompt: The wedding feast scene, but in the far distance a shadow falls across the landscape — a hint of something coming. The joy in the foreground is real, but there is depth behind it.

verses.kjv: And he spake also a parable unto them; No man putteth a piece of a new garment upon an old; if otherwise, then both the new maketh a rent, and the piece that was taken out of the new agreeth not with the old.
verses.little_bible: Jesus told them a parable: 'No one tears a patch from a brand-new coat and sews it onto an old one. If you do, you ruin the new coat — and the patch doesn't even match the old one anyway!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'Don't patch an old coat with new cloth — it will only make things worse!'
verses.meaning: You cannot partially update old ways of life with a little bit of Jesus. He doesn't come to patch — He comes to make us completely new. Half-measures don't work.
verses.memory_phrase: Jesus makes us completely new.
verses.prayer: Jesus, don't just patch my old life. Make me brand new from the inside out.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever tried to fix something with the wrong thing? What happened?
verses.family_discussion: What does it mean to try to 'patch' our lives with Jesus rather than being fully transformed by Him? Are there areas where your family has done this?
verses.do_it_today: Ask God to show you one area of your life where you need complete change — not just a patch.
verses.illustration_prompt: Two side-by-side illustrations: first, a bright new patch being sewn onto a worn old coat; second, the patch tearing away and leaving a bigger hole. Simple, clear storybook watercolour.

verses.kjv: And no man putteth new wine into old bottles; else the new wine will burst the bottles, and be spilled, and the bottles shall perish.
verses.little_bible: Jesus continued: 'And no one pours new wine into old dried-out wineskins. The new wine will expand and burst the old skins — and you'll lose both the wine and the wineskins!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'New wine in old wineskins will burst them — you lose everything!'
verses.meaning: New wine ferments and expands. Old, stiff wineskins can't stretch — they burst. New life in Christ cannot be squeezed into old rigid religious systems. Something has to give.
verses.memory_phrase: New life needs new capacity.
verses.prayer: God, stretch my heart and mind to hold all the new things You are doing in my life.
verses.discussion_question: Have you ever seen something burst because it had too much inside it?
verses.family_discussion: Are there old habits, traditions, or ways of thinking in your family that might be preventing you from receiving what God wants to pour into you?
verses.do_it_today: Let go of one old habit or thinking pattern that is not helping you grow closer to God.
verses.illustration_prompt: An old cracked leather wineskin with wine bursting through splits in the seams, liquid spilling onto the ground. A new wineskin beside it, full and sealed. Clear contrast, storybook style.

verses.kjv: But new wine must be put into new bottles; and both are preserved.
verses.little_bible: Jesus said: 'New wine must go into new wineskins — and when it does, both the wine and the wineskins are kept safe!'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'New wine in new wineskins — then both are safe!'
verses.meaning: The solution is not to abandon wine or wineskins — it is to have the right container for what God is pouring. A heart renewed by Jesus can hold the life He brings.
verses.memory_phrase: A new heart holds new life.
verses.prayer: God, give me a new heart that is ready to hold all You want to pour into me.
verses.discussion_question: What do you think a new heart feels like?
verses.family_discussion: How does God make us into 'new wineskins'? What does that renewal process look like in a person's life?
verses.do_it_today: Write this prayer somewhere: 'God, I am willing to be made new. Pour into me.'
verses.illustration_prompt: A brand new wineskin, supple and full of rich red wine — sealed and intact. The wineskin glows with health and the wine is safe inside. A sense of wholeness and abundance.

verses.kjv: No man also having drunk old wine straightway desireth new: for he saith, The old is better.
verses.little_bible: Jesus finished with this thought: 'And no one who has been drinking old wine immediately wants new wine. They always say: "The old is better." It takes time to want something new.'
verses.little_reader_adaptation: Jesus said: 'People used to old wine always say: the old is better. Change is hard.'
verses.meaning: Jesus ended with a word of understanding — people resist change, especially when the familiar feels comfortable. The religious leaders were attached to the old. But what Jesus offers is better, even if it takes time to taste it.
verses.memory_phrase: Be open to the new thing Jesus brings.
verses.prayer: Jesus, help me not to be stuck on the old and comfortable if You are doing something new. Help me be open.
verses.discussion_question: Is there something new you were afraid to try but then loved? What was it?
verses.family_discussion: The religious leaders preferred what was familiar. How do we stay open to new things God wants to do — in our faith, our church, our family — without losing what is truly good from the past?
verses.do_it_today: Try one new thing today — in prayer, in how you read the Bible, or in how you serve someone. Be open.
verses.illustration_prompt: An older man sitting contentedly with a cup of dark old wine, eyes closed, smiling — satisfied and unchanged. Beside him, a younger figure reaching toward a new wineskin. Warm earthy colours, peaceful contrast.
